ethics: No pressure, no shame, no punitive streak mechanics.
Content should remain child-safe, everyday and emotionally appropriate.

hearing_support: Chunk-level visual support for all core prompts.
Optional slower playback with pauses and repeat-per-chunk.
Non-audio feedback cues such as vibration or animation.
vision_support: High-contrast UI and large tap targets.

evaluation_heuristic.name: pattern_match_score
notes: Accept short forms and meaning-preserving variants.
Deduct only when the pattern no longer serves the intended communicative function.
Transfer items may pass with one missing helper word if the intent remains clear.

exercises.prompt_l1: Dekodiere den Satz in Chunks: Good morning, I am Mia.
exercises.prompt_l2: Decode the sentence in chunks: Good morning, I am Mia.
exercises.audio_hint: Slow, warm voice, 2 full repeats, then 1 chunked repeat with pauses.
assistive_hints: Show the full sentence before tapping chunks.
Point to the speaker while hearing I am.
Allow I am and I'm as equivalent in later rounds.

exercises.prompt_l1: Hoere nebenbei: Open the book, please.
exercises.prompt_l2: Listen in the background: Open the book, please.
exercises.audio_hint: Very clear child-friendly voice, 5 calm loops, slight pause after open.
assistive_hints: Pair with a real book movement.
No speaking pressure during the first loops.
Keep volume low and non-intrusive.

exercises.prompt_l1: Sage: My name is Leo. I am from Berlin. Ersetze Name und Ort durch eigene Angaben.
exercises.prompt_l2: Say: My name is Leo. I am from Berlin. Replace name and place with your own.
exercises.audio_hint: Model once slowly, once at natural speed, then leave a response gap.
assistive_hints: Offer a sentence frame on screen.
Accept one-part answers first, then invite the second part.
Use visual cards for name and city.

exercises.prompt_l1: Aendere das Muster fuer dich: I am happy. I need water.
exercises.prompt_l2: Change the pattern for yourself: I am happy. I need water.
exercises.audio_hint: Two expressive models with emotion change, then one silent response gap.
assistive_hints: Swap only the feeling or the need, not both at once.
Use picture cards for happy, tired, water, help.
Meaning is more important than perfect pronunciation.

exercises.prompt_l1: Dekodiere: This is my brother. He is ten.
exercises.prompt_l2: Decode: This is my brother. He is ten.
exercises.audio_hint: Slow first pass, then normal pace, small pause between the two sentences.
assistive_hints: Show one family photo or drawing.
Highlight my as ownership, not as an isolated grammar term.
Accept sister as a transfer variation after decoding.

exercises.prompt_l1: Hoere entspannt: I like apples. I do not like milk.
exercises.prompt_l2: Listen calmly: I like apples. I do not like milk.
exercises.audio_hint: Gentle contrastive stress on like and do not like, 4 loops.
assistive_hints: Use food pictures, not word lists.
Keep the negative pattern as one chunk.
Later invite a personal variant with one new food.

exercises.prompt_l1: Antworte mit zwei Teilen: I like music, but I do not like math.
exercises.prompt_l2: Answer in two parts: I like music, but I do not like math.
exercises.audio_hint: One model with clear contrast, then learner response time.
assistive_hints: Offer two school subject icons to choose from.
If needed, remove but and practice one sentence first.
Reward meaningful contrast, not accent perfection.

exercises.prompt_l1: Uebertrage das Muster: The ball is under the chair. Tausche Objekt oder Ort.
exercises.prompt_l2: Transfer the pattern: The ball is under the chair. Change the object or the place.
exercises.audio_hint: Natural classroom voice, 2 models with gesture to the correct place.
assistive_hints: Change only one variable first: ball to book or chair to table.
Use real objects in the room.
Meaningful preposition use matters more than article accuracy.

exercises.prompt_l1: Dekodiere: I get up at seven and go to school by bus.
exercises.prompt_l2: Decode: I get up at seven and go to school by bus.
exercises.audio_hint: Steady rhythm with a small pause before and, then one full fluent version.
assistive_hints: Animate the routine with icons.
Keep time as a chunk, not as isolated numbers practice.
Invite a personal time change only after comprehension is stable.

exercises.prompt_l1: Hoere mehrmals: Go straight, then turn left at the park.
exercises.prompt_l2: Listen several times: Go straight, then turn left at the park.
exercises.audio_hint: Clear directional emphasis, 5 loops, with a short map animation if available.
assistive_hints: Use a simple map with one landmark.
Do not add right until left is stable.
Later ask the learner to trace the path silently while listening.

exercises.prompt_l1: Sprich mit Satzrahmen: On Saturday I play football with my friends.
exercises.prompt_l2: Speak with the frame: On Saturday I play football with my friends.
exercises.audio_hint: Model once with natural joy, then leave a long answer gap.
assistive_hints: Swap Saturday or the activity first.
Use hobby cards to reduce search load.
Accept singular friend if meaning stays clear.

exercises.prompt_l1: Nutze das Muster in neuem Kontext: How much is this T-shirt? It is twelve pounds.
exercises.prompt_l2: Use the pattern in a new context: How much is this T-shirt? It is twelve pounds.
exercises.audio_hint: Two-speaker model, shopper and clerk, medium pace.
assistive_hints: Change product before changing price.
Numbers can be supported visually.
If needed, accept euros with the same pattern.

exercises.prompt_l1: Dekodiere: Yesterday we visited grandma and played cards.
exercises.prompt_l2: Decode: Yesterday we visited grandma and played cards.
exercises.audio_hint: Gentle storytelling voice, slight pause after yesterday.
assistive_hints: Treat we visited as one usable past chunk.
Support with a simple picture sequence.
Do not explain past tense labels unless asked.

exercises.prompt_l1: Hoere wiederholt: Tomorrow I am going to the museum with my class.
exercises.prompt_l2: Listen repeatedly: Tomorrow I am going to the museum with my class.
exercises.audio_hint: Soft anticipatory tone, 4 loops, slight lift on tomorrow.
assistive_hints: Keep am going to as one future-planning chunk.
Use a calendar icon for tomorrow.
Later allow the learner to swap museum for zoo or park.

exercises.prompt_l1: Antworte kurz und klar: My head hurts. I need water and a short break.
exercises.prompt_l2: Answer briefly and clearly: My head hurts. I need water and a short break.
exercises.audio_hint: Calm supportive voice, model once, then a response gap for imitation or adaptation.
assistive_hints: Offer pain icons for head, stomach, throat.
Keep the request formula constant while changing the problem.
Functional clarity matters more than full sentence length.

exercises.prompt_l1: Veraendere das Muster: I missed the bus, so I will call my mother.
exercises.prompt_l2: Change the pattern: I missed the bus, so I will call my mother.
exercises.audio_hint: Short dramatic problem-solution contrast, 2 modeled versions.
assistive_hints: Change only the problem first: bus to train, book, key.
Keep so as the bridge from problem to action.
Accept gonna only in advanced repetitions, not as the base form.

exercises.prompt_l1: Dekodiere: I prefer the train because it is faster and less stressful.
exercises.prompt_l2: Decode: I prefer the train because it is faster and less stressful.
exercises.audio_hint: Balanced explanatory tone, clear stress on prefer and because.
assistive_hints: Keep because attached to a reason chunk.
Allow easier adjective swaps such as cheaper or easier.
Do not force full accuracy on comparative forms in the first round.

exercises.prompt_l1: Hoere die Reihenfolge mehrfach: First we packed our bags, then we drove to the lake, and later we had a picnic.
exercises.prompt_l2: Listen to the sequence several times: First we packed our bags, then we drove to the lake, and later we had a picnic.
exercises.audio_hint: Story pace with clear sequence markers, 3 loops, no rush.
assistive_hints: Show a 3-step picture strip.
Sequence markers are the main target, not every past form.
Invite silent pointing to the correct picture order while listening.

exercises.prompt_l1: Begruende deine Wahl: I think the blue backpack is better for school because it has more space.
exercises.prompt_l2: Give a reason for your choice: I think the blue backpack is better for school because it has more space.
exercises.audio_hint: Natural persuasive tone with a long pause for learner choice.
assistive_hints: Offer two visible options only.
A short reason is enough if the choice pattern is correct.
Allow color or size changes while keeping the reason frame.

exercises.prompt_l1: Uebertrage das Hilfsmuster: Could you say that again more slowly? I understood the first part, but not the last word.
exercises.prompt_l2: Transfer the support pattern: Could you say that again more slowly? I understood the first part, but not the last word.

decode_map.l1: koenntest du das noch einmal sagen

exercises.audio_hint: Helpful peer-to-peer tone, 2 versions: polite and slightly more urgent.
assistive_hints: This pattern protects communication and should be overlearned.
Swap last word with last sentence only after the base frame is stable.
Reward repair behavior, not just grammar accuracy.

exercises.prompt_l1: Dekodiere: If you want to learn faster, listen first and speak in short patterns.
exercises.prompt_l2: Decode: If you want to learn faster, listen first and speak in short patterns.
exercises.audio_hint: Confident coaching tone, one slow version and one natural version.
assistive_hints: Keep the advice structure as one chunked sequence.
This item can bridge pedagogy and learner reflection.
Allow slower or better instead of faster in transfer rounds.

exercises.prompt_l1: Hoere den Grund-Zusammenhang: We need to change the meeting because my brother has a doctor appointment.
exercises.prompt_l2: Listen to the cause-and-change relation: We need to change the meeting because my brother has a doctor appointment.
exercises.audio_hint: Calm planning voice, 4 loops, emphasis on the reason link.
assistive_hints: Use a calendar icon and one family icon.
Keep because visible during listening.
Later switch brother to mother, teacher or friend.

exercises.prompt_l1: Erzaehle kurz mit Wendepunkt: Last week I felt nervous, but then I practiced every day and became more confident.
exercises.prompt_l2: Tell it briefly with a turning point: Last week I felt nervous, but then I practiced every day and became more confident.
exercises.audio_hint: Reflective tone, one full model, then generous response time.
assistive_hints: Let the learner replace nervous with another real feeling.
Keep the turning point but then in place.
A shorter personal version still counts if the growth pattern is clear.

exercises.prompt_l1: Nutze Meinung plus Plan: In my opinion, children should play outside more often because they learn teamwork and stay active. This week I want to plan one outdoor game.
exercises.prompt_l2: Use opinion plus plan: In my opinion, children should play outside more often because they learn teamwork and stay active. This week I want to plan one outdoor game.

decode_map.l2: This week I want to plan

expected_patterns: this week i want to plan
exercises.audio_hint: Persuasive but friendly tone, split into opinion sentence and plan sentence.
assistive_hints: If needed, keep only one reason instead of two.
Change the activity before changing the whole opinion frame.
This is a high-support B1 transfer item, not a free essay.
